assertions.description: navigator.storage is exposed — the StorageManager surface this change tightens.
StorageManager.estimate() is a function on the prototype.
estimate() resolves to a StorageEstimate dictionary with numeric `quota` and `usage` fields.
assertions.test: (async()=>{const e=await navigator.storage.estimate();return typeof e.quota==='number' && typeof e.usage==='number';})()

assertions.description: Per the bug-fix shape, the quota in non-unlimited contexts is bucketed to at least 10 GiB plus usage; this checks the floor invariant the spec text describes.
navigator.storage.persisted exists alongside estimate() — both methods are part of the StorageManager surface this feature touches.
After the fix, calling estimate() twice in succession returns a stable quota value — no jitter, no per-call surface that exposes available RAM.
